skill.name: azure-keyvault-keys-ts
skill.description: Manage cryptographic keys using Azure Key Vault Keys SDK for JavaScript (@azure/keyvault-keys). Use when creating, encrypting/decrypting, signing, or rotating keys.
skill.summary: Manage cryptographic keys using Azure Key Vault Keys SDK for JavaScript/TypeScript. Provides key creation, encryption, signing, rotation, and backup operations.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 47 potential issues, but all are false positives. The file is legitimate Azure SDK documentation containing example code blocks (not actual executables), placeholder URLs (not real endpoints), and standard environment variable patterns (Azure best practice). No malicious behavior detected.

critical_findings.title: False Positive: Critical Heuristic Detection
critical_findings.description: Scanner detected 'dangerous combination: code execution + network + credential access' but this is triggered by documentation patterns. The file contains example SDK code showing how to authenticate to Azure Key Vault - this is expected documentation behavior, not malicious activity.
critical_findings.confidence_reasoning: The heuristic was triggered by markdown code blocks containing network URLs and environment variable access - exactly what legitimate Azure SDK documentation should contain.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: External Commands Detection
medium_findings.description: Scanner flagged 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' at 38 locations. This is a markdown documentation file where backticks are used for code fences (```bash, ```typescript). No actual shell execution code exists.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file uses markdown code fences for documentation examples. The backticks are markdown syntax, not shell backtick operators.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: Hardcoded URL Detection
medium_findings.description: Scanner flagged hardcoded URLs at lines 23 and 35. These are placeholder template URLs (https://<vault-name>.vault.azure.net) and dynamic URL construction from environment variables - standard Azure SDK patterns.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 23 shows a template URL with placeholder <vault-name>. Line 35 constructs URL from process.env.AZURE_KEYVAULT_NAME - this is the documented Azure SDK authentication pattern.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: Environment Variable Access
medium_findings.description: Scanner flagged environment variable access at line 35. Accessing AZURE_KEYVAULT_NAME via process.env is the standard, documented way to configure Azure SDKs. This is not a security vulnerability.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: process.env.AZURE_KEYVAULT_NAME is the documented Azure SDK pattern for specifying the vault name. No secrets are exposed in this file.

content.value_statement: Need to create, encrypt, sign, or rotate cryptographic keys in Azure? This skill provides ready-to-use code snippets for the Azure Key Vault Keys SDK to handle key management securely.
actual_capabilities: Create RSA and elliptic curve keys in Azure Key Vault
Encrypt and decrypt data using Azure Key Vault cryptography clients
Sign and verify digital signatures with managed keys
Rotate keys manually or automatically with rotation policies
Backup and restore keys across vaults
List, get, and delete keys with proper error handling
limitations: Only supports Node.js runtime (browser not supported)
Requires Azure subscription and configured Key Vault
Does not support on-premises key management
Depends on network connectivity to Azure

use_cases.title: Cloud Application Developer
use_cases.description: Implement encryption for data at rest in cloud applications using Azure Key Vault for secure key storage and management.

use_cases.title: DevOps Engineer
use_cases.description: Manage key rotation policies and automate cryptographic key lifecycle operations in Azure environments.

use_cases.title: Security Engineer
use_cases.description: Implement proper key management workflows including backup, restore, and access controls for compliance.

prompt_templates.title: Create a new encryption key
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new RSA key named 'my-encryption-key' in Azure Key Vault with 2048-bit size. Enable the key and set an expiration date.
prompt_templates.scenario: Need to generate a new key for data encryption

prompt_templates.title: Encrypt data with managed key
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to encrypt a user password using the Azure Key Vault cryptography client with RSA-OAEP algorithm.

prompt_templates.title: Set up automatic key rotation
prompt_templates.prompt: Configure automatic rotation for 'my-rotation-key' so it rotates every 90 days. Show the rotation policy setup.

prompt_templates.title: Backup and restore a key
prompt_templates.prompt: How do I backup a key from one Azure Key Vault and restore it to a different vault? Include the backup and restore operations.

output_examples.input: Create an EC key named 'signing-key' using P-256 curve

best_practices: Use DefaultAzureCredential for automatic authentication across dev and production environments
Enable soft-delete on vaults to protect against accidental key deletion
Set expiration dates on keys and implement rotation policies for security compliance
anti_patterns: Hardcoding connection strings or secrets in source code - use Azure Key Vault or environment variables
Using keys without expiration dates - always set validity periods
Disabling key operations logging - audit trail is critical for security compliance

faq.question: What authentication methods does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill uses DefaultAzureCredential which supports managed identities, service principals, and developer credentials automatically.

faq.question: Can this skill work with on-premises key vaults?
faq.answer: No, Azure Key Vault is a cloud service. For on-premises, consider HashiCorp Vault or similar solutions.

faq.question: Is this skill compatible with browser applications?
faq.answer: No, the Azure Key Vault SDK is Node.js only. Browser applications should use token-based authentication with a backend service.

faq.question: How do I handle key rotation in production?
faq.answer: Use the updateKeyRotationPolicy method to set automatic rotation. The rotateKey method can also be called manually or via automation.

faq.question: What key types are supported?
faq.answer: RSA (2048, 3072, 4096-bit), EC (P-256, P-384, P-521 curves), and AES symmetric keys are supported.

faq.question: Can I restore a key to a different vault?
faq.answer: Yes, the restoreKeyBackup method accepts backups from any vault, enabling cross-vault key migration.
